bullets.text: Full-year CR 90.9 (vs. 90.6 in 2018); ROE 31.3%; Commercial Lines CR 89.6; Property CR improved from 106.9 to 101.7 but still above target; $214.5M retained wind/hail losses added 13.8 points to Property loss ratio.

NPW grew 15% to $37.6 billion (+$5B YOY); Personal Lines NPW +15%; Commercial Lines NPW +20% to ~$4.8B; Property NPW +16% to $1.7B; five consecutive years of Direct auto new quote and new application growth.

Personal auto PIF growth 11%; Property PIFs up 14% to 2.2 million; Robinson (auto+home bundle) customers growing; 30% of Commercial Lines policyholders also had Personal Lines policy; mobile app customer engagement up ~4x since 2016.

Investment portfolio exceeded $39 billion; full-year FTE return 7.9% (fixed income 6.0%, equity 30.5%); Board declared $2.25/share annual-variable dividend ($1.3B total) plus $0.10/share quarterly; new dividend policy gave more flexibility vs. prior Gainshare formula.

Property started reporting into Personal Lines in January 2020; ARX minority buyout agreed February 2020 (100% ownership, one year early); HomeQuote Explorer in 47 states; BOP/GL launched in 11 agency states; BusinessQuote Explorer with multiple carriers; Flo chatbot in Facebook Messenger.

Favorable frequency trends in personal auto beyond model expectations; customer mix shifting toward lower pure premium risk; Snapshot data showing longer trips indicative of preferred driver behavior; Property wind/hail losses persistently above modeled load.

Flo/Superstore at 10 years with fresh story arcs; Motaur motorcycle campaign launched; Baker and Emily Mayfield 'At Home' homeowner campaign; four different campaigns all showing success; impressions against homeowner/bundle segment more than doubled vs. 2015.

NAER trimmed 3 points over 10 years; LAE ratio down over 1 point in 10 years; Personal Lines expense ratio consistently below 20 points; carbon emissions per policy down 7% in 2019 vs. 2018 and down 57% over past decade.

Added 4,000+ employees in 2019; Board reached 50/50 gender balance; purpose statement 'True to our name. Progressive.' adopted by employee vote; first corporate sustainability report published at progressive.com/sustainability.

Progressive estimated personal auto market share ~10% (+0.5 pts); estimated ~10 points more profitable than industry in private passenger auto; #1 in private passenger auto in 7 states, #2 in 19 more; Commercial Lines wrote >$4B NPW.
